Using XGPImage

This article will describe how to create a DSP effects image on the command line using XGPImage. For information on building DSP images using a graphical user interface, see DSP Builder.

  • Prerequisites and Requirements
  • Implementation Steps
  • Related Topics

Prerequisites and Requirements

You will need the XgpImage.exe tool that is included in the XDK. You will also need the binary images of the DSP program code for the effects you wish to use. The binary images of the actual DSP program code for the effects currently provided in the XDK can be found in the \source\dsound\dsp\bin directory of the Xbox SDK. The .ini files for those binary images can be found in the \source\dsound\dsp\ini directory.

Implementation Steps

Complete the following steps to create a DSP effects image.

  1. Determine the effects you wish to use in your DSP effects image. For this tutorial, we will be using the chorus and echo effects provided in the XDK, so we will need the echo.scr, echostate.ini, Chorus_stereo.scr, and Chorus_stereostate.ini files.
  2. Open the .ini file for the image you wish to create. For this tutorial, call it MyEffects.ini.
  3. Create the MAIN section of the .ini file.
    4. [MAIN]
  4. Specify the friendly name that will be used to create the output .h file for referencing the effects using DirectSound. For this tutorial, call it MyEffectsImage.
    5. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
  5. Specify the number of temporary mixbin locations to allocate for the entire set of effects chains. For this tutorial, specify one.
    6. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
  6. Complete the following steps for each DSP program chain graph.
    1. Specify the graph keyword. Each keyword consists of a the word GRAPH and a number. The number must be in sequence with any other graph keywords specified and the first one must start with zero. The maximum value that can be used for the graph number is 20. Prefix the number with GRAPH and add it to the .ini file. Because this is the first one, it should be number zero.
      2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0
    2. Specify a graph name. For this tutorial, call it ECHO.
      3. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO

    For this tutorial, add a second graph called CHAIN. Because it's the second graph in this .ini file, it should be assigned number one.

    [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN

    Also, all effect images should include the crosstalk cancellation effect, so let's add a third graph, CROSSTALK, into our .ini file.

    [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK
  7. Complete the following steps for each DSP program chain graph specified above.
    1. Create a section for the graph. The name of the section must match the name used above. For the first graph (GRAPH0), the name would be ECHO.
      2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
    2. Complete the following steps for each effect in this graph.
      1. Specify the effect keyword. The keyword consists of the letters FX and a number. The number must be in sequence with any other effect keywords specified and the first one must start with zero. Prefix the number with FX and add it to the .ini file. Because this is the first one, it should be number zero.
        2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0
      2. Specify the name of the effect to be used. For this tutorial, specify the ECHO effect.
        3. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

    Because the example has three graphs, we need to create a few more sections, CHAIN and CROSSTALK, and add some effects. For the CHAIN graph, add two effects: FLANGE and AMPMOD. For the CROSSTALK graph, add XTALK.

    [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK
  8. Complete the following steps for each effect specified.
    1. Specify the section name. The section name is a combination of the graph name, effect keyword, and effect name. For the first graph in the example, the graph name would be ECHO, the effect keyword would be FX0, and the effect name would be ECHO, so the section name would be ECHO_FX0_ECHO.
      2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
    2. Specify whether or not the output of the effect should be mixed into its output buffer or if it should overwrite the buffer. This is done using the keyword FX_MIXOUTPUT and the values zero and one. Zero indicates that the output should not be mixed; one indicates that the output should be mixed. For this effect, overwrite the buffer.
      3. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
    3. Specify the location of the binary image of the effect using the FX_DSPCODE keyword. In this case, it's probably something like C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr.
      4. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
    4. Specify the location of the .ini file for the effect using the FX_DSPSTATE keyword. In this case, it's probably something like C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini.
      5. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
    5. Specify the number of inputs for this effect using the FX_NUMINPUTS keyword. For this example, specify one input.
      6. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
    6. Specify the number of outputs for this effect using the FX_NUMOUTPUTS keyword. For this example, specify one output.
      7. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
    7. Complete the following steps for each input specified using the FX_NUMINPUTS keyword.
      1. Specify the keyword for the input. Each keyword consists of the word FX_INPUT and a number. The number must be in sequence with any other inputs specified, and the first one must start with zero. Prefix the number with FX_INPUT and add it to the .ini file. Because this is the first one, it should be number zero.
        2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0
      2. Set the keyword equal to one of the constants for effect sources. For this example, use VPMIXBIN_FXSEND0, which is one of the user-definable effects send mixbins. For a list of the constants, see the Defining Effect Sources and Destinations section of the GP DSP Image Builder (xgpimage) article.
        3. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND0
    8. Complete the following steps for each input specified using the FX_NUMOUTPUTS keyword.
      1. Specify the keyword for the output. Each keyword consists of the word FX_OUTPUT and a number. The number must be in sequence with any other outputs specified, and the first one must start with zero. Prefix the number with FX_OUTPUT and add it to the .ini file. Because this is the first one, it should be number zero.
        2. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND0
FX_OUTPUT0
      2. Set the keyword equal to one of the constants for effect sources. For this example, use GPMIXBIN_FRONTLEFT, which is the output designated for the front left speaker. For a list of the constants, see the Defining Effect Sources and Destinations section of the GP DSP Image Builder (xgpimage) article.
        3. [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND0
FX_OUTPUT0=GPMIXBIN_FRONTLEFT

    Now fill in the information for the other graphs.

    Note    For chained effects, like the CHAIN graph in the example, the FX_MIXOUTPUT value for the first effect in the chain must be set to zero. If you don't do this, the effect may continue to mix silence with its previous data and create undesired sounds.

    [MAIN]
IMAGE_FRIENDLY_NAME=MyEffectsImage
FX_NUMTEMPBINS=1
GRAPH0=ECHO
GRAPH1=CHAIN
GRAPH2=CROSSTALK

[ECHO]
FX0=ECHO

[CHAIN]
FX0=FLANGE
FX1=AMPMOD

[CROSSTALK]
FX0=XTALK

[ECHO_FX0_ECHO]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\echo.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\ini\echostate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=1
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND0
FX_OUTPUT0=GPMIXBIN_FRONTLEFT

[CHAIN_FX0_FLANGE]

FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\flange.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\ini\flangestate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=2
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND1
FX_INPUT1=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND2
FX_OUTPUT0=GPTEMPBIN0

[CHAIN_FX1_AMPMOD]

FX_MIXOUTPUT=0
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\ampmod.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\ini\ampmodstate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=2
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=1
FX_INPUT0=GPTEMPBIN0
FX_INPUT1=VPMIXBIN_FXSEND4
FX_OUTPUT0=GPMIXBIN_FXSEND19

[CROSSTALK_FX0_XTALK]
FX_MIXOUTPUT=1
FX_DSPCODE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\bin\xtalk.scr
FX_DSPSTATE=C:\Program Files\Microsoft Xbox SDK\source\dsound\dsp\ini\xtalkstate.ini
FX_NUMINPUTS=4
FX_NUMOUTPUTS=4
FX_INPUT0=VPMIXBIN_XTLK_FR
FX_INPUT1=VPMIXBIN_XTLK_FL
FX_INPUT2=VPMIXBIN_XTLK_BL
FX_INPUT3=VPMIXBIN_XTLK_BR
FX_OUTPUT0=GPMIXBIN_FRONTRIGHT
FX_OUTPUT1=GPMIXBIN_FRONTLEFT
FX_OUTPUT2=GPMIXBIN_BACKLEFT
FX_OUTPUT3=GPMIXBIN_BACKRIGHT
  9. Use the XgpImage.exe tool to create the image. For this example, use MyImage.bin as the name of the binary image that the tool will produce and MyImage.h as the header file the tool will create. The call to the tool on the command line should like something like this:
    10. xgpimage MyEffects.ini MyImage.bin MyImage.h

Related Topics

The following topics are related to creating a DSP effects image.

  • DSP Effects Processing
  • XGP DSP Image Builder (xgpimage)
